user.title: Sign in to Divoom
user.description: One-time sign-in to enumerate devices and fetch each LocalToken. Only UserId, cloud Token, and per-device LocalToken are stored — not your password.

pick_device.title: Pick a device
pick_device.description: Times Gate speaks MQTT (real-time state, low latency); Times Frame speaks HTTP only. The next step depends on which one you pick.

broker.title: MQTT broker for {name}
broker.description: Enter the LAN IP of the MQTT broker your Times Gate should connect to (usually the same one Home Assistant uses). I'll register it with the Divoom cloud via App/SetIp; the device joins on its next poll.

reauth_confirm.title: Sign in again
reauth_confirm.description: The stored token was rejected. Re-enter your Divoom credentials; I'll refresh both the cloud token (for MQTT auth) and the LocalToken (for HTTP).

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the Divoom cloud.
error.no_devices_found: No Divoom devices found on this account.
error.unknown_device: That device is no longer on the account.
error.mqtt_not_configured: This device needs MQTT, but Home Assistant's MQTT integration isn't set up.
error.app_set_ip_failed: Divoom cloud refused App/SetIp — check the broker IP.
